lines.speaker: Man
lines.text: Bula, bro! Have you played rugby?

lines.speaker: Man
lines.text: This is Albert Park. The most famous sports ground in Fiji.

lines.speaker: Man
lines.text: First time in Suva? Follow Victoria Parade and you'll see Parliament and the Grand Pacific Hotel.

lines.speaker: Man
lines.text: Fijians love rugby, seriously. Want to play a round? haha

lines.speaker: Man
lines.text: Ah, you're from KODA! Working on the Naitamba project, right?

lines.speaker: Man
lines.text: Rugby is the same - teamwork is everything, bro.

lines.speaker: Man
lines.text: If you want to work well with island people, don't charge ahead alone. You have to pass.

lines.speaker: Man
lines.text: I learned that from rugby, really. haha

lines.speaker: Woman
lines.text: Bula vinaka!

lines.speaker: Woman
lines.text: Nice weather today. Good thing it isn't raining.

lines.speaker: Woman
lines.text: Are you from Naitamba Island? I have relatives there too.

lines.speaker: Woman
lines.text: Bula! Where are you from?

lines.speaker: Woman
lines.text: First time in Fiji? People here are kind.

lines.speaker: Woman
lines.text: Vinaka vakalevu means thank you very much. Good phrase to remember. haha

lines.speaker: Vendor
lines.text: Bula bro! Welcome to Pacific City.

lines.speaker: Vendor
lines.text: We sell everything here. But if you're going to an island village, come back.

lines.speaker: Vendor
lines.text: When you first meet a village elder, it's proper to bring yaqona. Sevusevu - heard of it?

lines.speaker: Vendor
lines.text: That Naitamba Island project - I hope it happens soon. My friend lives there.

lines.speaker: Vendor
lines.text: Bula bro! What are you looking for in Pacific City?

lines.speaker: Vendor
lines.text: Visiting a village? Then you need a gift. What will it be?

lines.speaker: Vendor
lines.text: Root! Good choice, bro.

lines.speaker: Vendor
lines.text: Village elders prefer the traditional way. You pound it yourself for sevusevu.

lines.speaker: Vendor
lines.text: I'll pick a good one for you. Here. Vinaka!
You packed the yaqona root.

lines.speaker: Vendor
lines.text: Powder? It is convenient, but...

lines.speaker: Vendor
lines.text: Honestly, village elders prefer root over powder.

lines.speaker: Vendor
lines.text: Still, better than nothing. Here you go.
You packed the yaqona powder.

lines.speaker: Vendor
lines.text: Mango? Tastes good. But...

lines.speaker: Vendor
lines.text: For sevusevu, yaqona is the proper gift. Mango is just a snack.

lines.speaker: Vendor
lines.text: Well, better than showing up empty-handed. Here you go.
You packed the mango.

lines.speaker: Vendor
lines.text: No worries. Come back later!

lines.speaker: Vendor
lines.text: Give them the yaqona well! Come back to Pacific City anytime, bro.

lines.speaker: Police Officer
lines.text: Bula! Welcome to Fiji.

lines.speaker: Police Officer
lines.text: Tell me if you need help.

lines.speaker: Police Officer
lines.text: There are some protesters near the government building these days. Be careful.

lines.speaker: Woman
lines.text: Namaste.

lines.speaker: Woman
lines.text: Where are you from?

lines.speaker: Woman
lines.text: Our shop is across the street. Stop by later and I'll make you tea.

lines.speaker: Man
lines.text: Good morning.

lines.speaker: Man
lines.text: You work for KODA? The Naitamba Island project, right?

lines.speaker: Man
lines.text: We Fijians know that island's story well.

lines.speaker: Man
lines.text: If you're talking to government... be careful with Vikash. He's difficult.

lines.speaker: Resident
lines.text: Bula!

lines.speaker: Resident
lines.text: Have a good day, bro.

lines.speaker: Man
lines.text: Did you meet Vikash? How was he?

lines.speaker: Man
lines.text: He has his reasons too. A project went badly on his watch before.

lines.speaker: Man
lines.text: Bring clean documents. He likes that.

lines.speaker: Police Officer
lines.text: Back from Naitamba?

lines.speaker: Police Officer
lines.text: Good people there. But be careful - if you break a promise, there won't be a second chance.

lines.speaker: Woman
lines.text: Bula! You look busy these days.

lines.speaker: Woman
lines.text: I heard you met people from the international organization too. Suva is small; news travels fast. haha

lines.speaker: Woman
lines.text: I hope it works out. Naitamba people have waited a long time.

lines.speaker: Elder
lines.text: Bula. You came from Suva?

lines.speaker: Elder
lines.text: Here they come again. People came before too, ten years ago.

lines.speaker: Elder
lines.text: They built a water tank, but no one maintained it, so we can't use it now.

lines.speaker: Elder
lines.text: Water does come out. But in the dry season the pressure gets weak and it is difficult.

lines.speaker: Elder
lines.text: We asked for repairs, but no one came from Suva. Ten years now.

lines.speaker: Elder
lines.text: I heard Ratu signed? That's a first.

lines.speaker: Elder
lines.text: Maybe this time is different...

lines.speaker: Child
lines.text: Bula! Where are you from?

lines.speaker: Child
lines.text: Suva? They say schools there are big. Our school is good too. Our teacher is fun.

lines.speaker: Child
lines.text: But sometimes the tap water doesn't come out. Then my mother worries.

lines.speaker: Child
lines.text: Do you like rugby? Someday I want to go to Suva and play at Albert Park!

lines.speaker: Young Man
lines.text: Bula. I work as a fisher.

lines.speaker: Young Man
lines.text: Ten years ago, I was excited when they said they would build a new tank.

lines.speaker: Young Man
lines.text: They built it, but there was no maintenance contract, so when pipes burst, we couldn't repair them.

lines.speaker: Young Man
lines.text: We asked Suva to send a technician, but they said there was no budget. That was ten years ago.

lines.speaker: Young Man
lines.text: If this happens the same way again, better to let the village manage it ourselves.

lines.speaker: Young Man
lines.text: The village will manage it directly? Brother Josua knows some technical work.

lines.speaker: Young Man
lines.text: If that happens, I'll help too. I can connect pipes at least.

lines.speaker: Woman
lines.text: Bula! I'm coming back from the market.

lines.speaker: Woman
lines.text: Life on this island is not bad. We have the sea and good people.

lines.speaker: Woman
lines.text: But the water system is old. When pressure drops in the dry season, it is inconvenient.

lines.speaker: Woman
lines.text: In Suva you don't worry about that, right? Here, infrastructure maintenance is slow.

lines.speaker: Woman
lines.text: If a real water tank comes... I won't have to worry about laundry.

lines.speaker: Woman
lines.text: It sounds like a dream. But people say this time is different.
